sections.title: National organizations
sections.blurb: Reputable national science and science-education organizations, selected for relevance to Georgia audiences.

items.name: National Science Teaching Association (NSTA)
items.description: Professional organization for science teachers, with classroom resources, standards guidance, and professional development.
items.rationale: A primary national source for vetted, standards-aligned science teaching materials.

items.name: National Center for Science Education (NCSE)
items.description: Defends the integrity of science education, with resources on teaching evolution and climate change accurately.
items.rationale: Directly supports integrity in how science is taught, a core part of our mission.

sections.title: Georgia-specific resources
sections.blurb: State standards, institutions, programs, and initiatives based in Georgia.

items.name: Georgia Department of Education, Science
items.description: Official Georgia Standards of Excellence for science, plus state curriculum guidance.
items.rationale: The authoritative source for what Georgia students are expected to learn in science.

items.name: Georgia Science Teachers Association (GSTA)
items.description: Statewide association supporting Georgia science teachers, with position statements and professional resources.
items.rationale: A Georgia-based professional voice for rigorous, evidence-based science instruction.

sections.title: For educators
sections.blurb: Classroom materials and STEM teaching support.

items.name: NASA STEM Engagement
items.description: Free, standards-aligned STEM lessons, activities, and multimedia from NASA.
items.rationale: High-quality, freely available classroom materials from a primary scientific institution.

sections.title: For students
sections.blurb: Explainers, study help, and clarification resources.

items.name: Khan Academy, Science
items.description: Free video lessons and practice across biology, chemistry, physics, and more.
items.rationale: Widely used, free, and well-structured for self-paced study and clarification.

sections.title: Critical thinking and media literacy
sections.blurb: Resources for evaluating evidence and navigating information.

items.name: News Literacy Project
items.description: Nonpartisan tools and lessons for telling credible information from misinformation.
items.rationale: Supports the evidence-evaluation skills at the heart of scientific literacy.
